title: Securing device for animal chew
abstract: The present invention is directed to a securing device that secures an animal chew to prevent animal choking. The body of the device surrounds a cavity and possesses two boreholes along an axis. A chew with one or more boreholes may be inserted into the cavity so that a borehole aligns along the axis. A pin is then inserted in the first passageway along the axis, until it passes through the borehole and into the second passageway. A latch obstructs the head of the pin so that the animal cannot work the pin free while enjoying the chew. To remove the pin, the animal's caretaker can apply force to the end of the pin to push it free past the latch. Optionally, the user may need to push aside the latch while pushing the pin free.
claims:
Claims I Claim: 1 A device for securing a chew, comprising: a body around a cavity; a first and second passageway in the body on an axis; wherein a chew is insertable into the cavity such that a borehole is on the axis; a pin, with a head and an insertion end, that is insertable along the axis through the borehole with the head in the first passageway; and a latch in the first passageway that is moveable between an engagement position that restricts removal of the pin, and a release position proximate to the head. 2 A device of claim 1 wherein the head is removeable from the first passageway when the latch is moved into the release position. 3 A device of claim 1 wherein the engaged pin cannot be removed from the first passageway until the latch is moved from the engagement position to the release position, while simultaneously force is applied to the insertion end. 4 A device of claim 1 wherein the engaged pin cannot be removed with a force less than 800 grams force on the insertion end until the latch in the first passageway is moved from the engagement position to the release position. 5 A device of claim 1 wherein the head and insertion end are functionally identical and the inserted pin is engaged by both latches. 6 A device of claim 1 wherein the first passageway and the second passageway are functionally identical. 7 A device of claim 1 wherein there is a latch fold zone in the first passageway that receives the latch when the latch is moved into the release position 8 A device of claim 1 further comprising a closed section coupled to the body on one side of the cavity. 9 A device of claim 1 wherein during pin insertion the head is automatically engaged by the latch in the first passageway. 10 A device of claim 1 wherein the cavity is accessible by the chew from either side of the body. 11 A device of claim 1 wherein the head is recessed within the first passageway, and the insertion end is recessed within the second passageway. 

 A device of claim 1 further comprising additional boreholes in the chew such that the chew is variably positionable to the device. A device for an animal chew comprising: a body around a cavity; a first passageway and a second passageway through the body on an axis; a chew with a borehole, wherein the chew is insertable into the cavity with the borehole on the axis; a pin that is insertable along the axis such that the pin insertion end passes through the first passageway, through the borehole, and ends in the second passageway, and the head is in the first passageway; and a latch in the first passageway is reversibly moveable between an engagement position with the head, and a bypass position with the head. A device of claim 13 wherein the engaged pin cannot be removed from the first passageway until the latch is moved from the engagement position to the release position, while simultaneously force is applied to the insertion end. A device of claim 13 wherein the first passageway and the second passageway are functionally identical. A method for a user to secure a chew with a borehole comprising: inserting a chew into a cavity that is surrounded by a body; aligning a borehole with an axis that passes though a first passageway and second passageway in the body; inserting a pin along the axis such that the insertion end passes through the first passageway, through the borehole, and ends in the second passageway, and the head is engaged by a moveable latch in the first passageway. A device of claim 16 wherein the user may select either passageway as the first passageway. A device of claim 16 wherein the user cannot comfortably remove the pin until they move the latch from an engagement position with the head to a release position proximate to the head while simultaneously applying force to the insertion end.
